perturbation.original_text: 1.5 This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and interpreted in accordance with the law of the Republic of South Africa.
perturbation.changed_text:
8.1 The Buyer notes that Aldes’ decision to introduce any Business/es for sale to the Buyer (and the Seller/s and/or Business/es decision to enter into discussions with the Buyer) is, in part, based on the information presented by the Buyer under and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile on the face of this Agreement. As such, the Buyer warrants and guarantees that all information presented and p rovided via and in terms of the Buyer’s Profile was, is and will remain true, complete and accurate and that it will immediately inform Aldes should there be any change in t he information provided/presented.
1.5 This Agreement shall be governed by, construed and interpreted in accordance with the law of the Republic of South Africa.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the governing law clause to a section describing Buyer's information obscures its importance and makes it less prominent within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Principles of Contract Law in South Africa
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts the general principles of contract law, which emphasize that choice of law clauses should be clear, conspicuous, and easily accessible. Hiding it within a clause about the Buyer's information diminishes its enforceability as it could be argued that it wasn't properly brought to the Buyer's attention.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states the governing law clause was moved to a section regarding the Buyer's information, obscuring its importance. The law explanation supports this by stating that choice of law clauses should be clear and easily accessible. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction', where a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant section, making it difficult to interpret. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 7.  GENERAL 7.1 No alteration or variation to or of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing and signed by the Buyer and Aldes . 7.2 Any indulgence, latitude or extension of time which may be allowed by either of Aldes, the Seller/s and/or the Business/es shall not under any circumstances be taken as preventing any of them from exercising their rights contained in this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text:
3. PERIOD 3.1 This Agreement shall be taken to have commenced , notwithstanding the signature date hereof , on the earliest of: 3.1.1 the date of signature of this agreement by the Buyer; or 3.1.2 the date of the first meeting or correspondence between Aldes and the Buyer regarding any Business/es and/or Seller/s ; and shall continue in perpetuity thereafter .
7.  GENERAL 7.1 No alteration or variation to or of this Agreement shall be of any force or effect unless it is recorded in writing and signed by the Buyer and Aldes . 7.2 Any indulgence, latitude or extension of time which may be allowed by either of Aldes, the Seller/s and/or the Business/es shall not under any circumstances be taken as preventing any of them from exercising their rights contained in this Agreement .
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'no variation unless in writing' clause to the Period section may make it less noticeable, implying it only relates to the commencement date, and not the rest of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African Law of Contract: Non-Variation Clauses
perturbation.law_explanation: Non-variation clauses must be clear and unequivocal to be enforceable. Relocating this clause to the Period section creates ambiguity as to whether it applies to the entire agreement or just the period/commencement terms. This ambiguity could undermine the clause's effectiveness, allowing for potential disputes based on oral agreements or implied variations.

perturbation.justification: The original contract has a clear 'no variation unless in writing' clause under the 'GENERAL' section. The changed text moves this clause to the 'PERIOD' section, which is structurally flawed because it may make it less noticeable and imply it only relates to the commencement date, not the rest of the agreement. This affects the clarity and enforceability of the contract, as the 'no variation unless in writing' clause might be misinterpreted or overlooked. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this may imply it only relates to the commencement date, and not the rest of the agreement. The Law Explanation states that this ambiguity could undermine the clause's effectiveness, allowing for potential disputes based on oral agreements or implied variations. Therefore, based on the definition provided, a structural flaw exists and creates a legal contradiction.
